title: A South Carolina Gator Was 'Posted Up Right at the Front Door'
script: Who's blocking YOUR front door? In Berkeley County, South Carolina, a homeowner stepped outside Tuesday and found an alligator — posted up right at the front door. Not in the yard. Not out back. The front door. Like it was waiting to be let in. Animal control was called to the scene. The gator appeared to be just... sitting there. Completely unbothered. No aggression, no apparent reason — it just picked that porch and claimed it. This isn't unusual for South Carolina. Gators wander in summer as they move between water sources, and Berkeley County has plenty. But most pick a bush, a ditch, a patch of grass. This one chose the porch. The entrance. The main event. So: did the alligator knock first?
